lessons.title: Build
lessons.paragraph: Lesson 1 creates the smallest working Go program for the project. The root main.go file belongs to package main and defines main(), which prints "Hello, World!" to the console. The project is initialized as the Go module pokedex-go, can be compiled with go build, and ignores the generated pokedex-go executable so build output does not get committed.

lessons.title: Tests
lessons.paragraph: Lesson 2 introduces test-driven development for cleanInput(text string) []string. The work began with a stub in repl.go and focused unit tests in repl_test.go covering whitespace trimming, splitting on any whitespace, lowercasing Pokemon names, tab and newline handling, and empty whitespace input; those tests failed against the stub before cleanInput was implemented with strings.TrimSpace, strings.ToLower, and strings.Fields. After formatting, go test ./..., go build, and running ./pokedex-go all passed.

lessons.title: REPL
lessons.paragraph: Lesson 3 replaces the one-time Hello World program with an interactive REPL entry point. main.go now calls startREPL(), which uses bufio.NewScanner(os.Stdin) to read each line, prints the prompt "Pokedex > ", cleans input through cleanInput, ignores empty commands, and echoes the first word as "Your command was: <command>". The existing cleanInput tests still pass, the project formats, tests, and builds successfully, and a piped run confirmed that mixed-case Pokemon sentences are normalized to first-word commands like charmander and pikachu while repl.log remains ignored.

lessons.title: Help and Exit
lessons.paragraph: Lesson 4 turns the REPL into a command-driven loop. repl.go now defines cliCommand with a name, description, and callback, getCommands() registers help and exit, and startREPL looks up the first cleaned word instead of echoing it, printing returned errors or Unknown command when no match exists. The help command prints a welcome message, usage text, and the stable command list, while exit prints "Closing the Pokedex... Goodbye!" before terminating; tests, build, and manual help, exit, and unknown-command runs all passed.

lessons.title: PokeAPI
lessons.paragraph: Lesson 5 connects the REPL to real Pokemon world data through the PokeAPI location-area endpoint. Command callbacks now receive a config pointer that stores next and previous pagination URLs, map fetches and prints the next 20 location area names, and mapb either prints "you're on the first page" or fetches the previous page. The help registry includes map and mapb, go test ./... and go build pass, and manual REPL runs verified first-page results, forward pagination, backtracking, and the updated help output.

lessons.title: Caching
lessons.paragraph: Lesson 6 adds an internal pokecache package for concurrency-safe in-memory response caching. The cache stores raw response bytes by URL, copies values on add and get, protects the map with a mutex, and starts a ticker-backed reap loop that removes entries older than the configured interval. PokeAPI location-area requests now check the cache before making HTTP calls and store successful responses afterward; package tests cover add/get and expiration, and a REPL-level test confirms repeated fetches for the same URL only hit HTTP once.

lessons.title: Explore
lessons.paragraph: Lesson 7 adds an explore command that accepts a location area name and fetches the matching PokeAPI location-area detail endpoint. The REPL now passes cleaned command arguments to callbacks, the shared cached fetch helper is reused for both map pagination and exploration, and explore prints an "Exploring <area>..." header followed by the Pokemon encounter list. Tests still pass, the cache behavior is covered for exploration, and a manual run of explore canalave-city-area printed Pokemon such as tentacool, staryu, magikarp, and gyarados.
